reviews.author: tip-bot2 Ilvokhin
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted that the patch introduces a new function __percpu_up_read() which is called from percpu_down_read_trylock(), but this function does not drop the lock before calling try_to_unmap(). The reviewer requested that the lock be dropped before calling try_to_unmap() to avoid potential issues with reclaim paths.

reviews.author: tip-bot2 Ilvokhin
reviews.summary: Reviewer Ilvokhin pointed out that the unnecessary include of linux/sched.h in tracing/lock.c was removed, as none of the trace events reference anything from this header.
reviews.raw_body:
The following commit has been merged into the locking/core branch of tip:

Commit-ID:     76080d81e511f66eeb094b204e1e217bff674922
Gitweb:        https://git.kernel.org/tip/76080d81e511f66eeb094b204e1e217bff674922
Author:        Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com>
AuthorDate:    Thu, 04 Jun 2026 07:15:05 
Committer:     Peter Zijlstra <peterz@infradead.org>
CommitterDate: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 13:41:24 +02:00

tracing/lock: Remove unnecessary linux/sched.h include

None of the trace events in lock.h reference anything from
linux/sched.h. Remove the unnecessary include.

Signed-off-by: Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com>
Signed-off-by: Peter Zijlstra (Intel) <peterz@infradead.org>
Acked-by: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>
Link: https://patch.msgid.link/85e14a0685c1d46c3e80de2dc01358a48169b263.1780506267.git.d@ilvokhin.com
---
 include/trace/events/lock.h | 1 -
 1 file changed, 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/include/trace/events/lock.h b/include/trace/events/lock.h
index 8e89baa..da978f2 100644
--- a/include/trace/events/lock.h
+++ b/include/trace/events/lock.h
@@ -5,7 +5,6 @@
 #if !defined(_TRACE_LOCK_H) || defined(TRACE_HEADER_MULTI_READ)
 #define _TRACE_LOCK_H
 
-#include <linux/sched.h>
 #include <linux/tracepoint.h>
 
 /* flags for lock:contention_begin */

reviews.author: tip-bot2 Ilvokhin
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted that the contended_release tracepoint fires after the lock is released and before the waiter is woken for some lock types, which may not be consistent across all lock types.

2026-06-12.patch_summary: This patch series adds a new tracepoint called contended_release that fires on the lock holder's side when a lock is released while there are waiters. This allows for more accurate measurement of hold time and provides additional information about the lock holder's stack at release time. The patch also addresses issues with existing contention_begin/contention_end tracepoints, which only work for certain types of locks.
